signal: Project check cron needed two-stage dirty detection, log removal, timeout increase — human coordinated fix
action: Cron prompt rewritten with observation→fix two-stage mechanism, timeout 120→240s, log writing removed
notes: 5 cycles without recurrence. Two-stage mechanism confirmed working across all runs 03-09. Job was deleted (ct_009) but recreated with identical config.

signal: Weather broadcast cron persistent timeout — thinking field contamination causes LLM hang
action: Fix #4 (evt_108): Definitive fix — delete+recreate job WITHOUT thinking field entirely. Previous fixes (#1 prompt, #2 provider, #3 thinking:off→low) all failed because cron patch cannot remove fields and any thinking value (off/low/none) causes FailoverError:LLM request timed out. New job abe78dcc has zero thinking field. Old orphan f542a607 also cleaned up.
notes: 4TH FIX ATTEMPT. Root cause: cron patch cannot delete fields via null, so thinking kept contaminating. The only way to remove it is delete+recreate. Evidence: all non-thinking jobs complete in 11-16s. Manual test triggered on new clean job.

signal: Heartbeat stale alerts flooding — config.patch restarts cause false positives
action: Added age-based stale detection (>35min = stale, no alert), gwup downgraded to info-only
notes: 5 cycles, 30+ heartbeat ticks since fix, zero false positives. Converged.

signal: Version update cron poor quality — hallucinated version count, announce delivery silently failing
action: Prompt rewritten with structured format, dedup mechanism, anti-hallucination rules, model switched to sonnet-4-6, explicit message tool calls instead of announce
notes: 5 cycles, multiple runs all ok (18-47s), no hallucinations, messages delivered correctly. Converged.

signal: PCEC produced 6+ consecutive skips while daily logs contained major human-intervention signals — skip epidemic
action: PCEC v3 rewrite: daily logs as primary signal, convergence tracking, no-skip mandate
notes: 6 consecutive productive cycles (evt_100-105). Skip epidemic eliminated by design.

signal: Timeout standardization was ad-hoc — each cron had inconsistent timeouts causing failures
notes: 5 cycles, zero timeout errors since standardization. Only weather pre-fix error was from before this change. Converged.

signal: wechat-mp-publisher v0.1.0 missing from README.md and README_CN.md — discovered by ACP Codex audit but never fixed
action: Added wechat-mp-publisher to both README tables
notes: One-time fix, committed (2a1b2e5). No recurrence possible. Converged.

signal: Consecutive config.patch creates duplicate heartbeat timers; cron patch cannot delete fields via null
action: Grounded both lessons to memory/reference/config-patch-behavior.md with symptoms, fixes, and prevention rules
notes: 3 cycles since grounding (evt_103-106), no recurrence of config.patch misuse. Knowledge successfully applied.

signal: Project check cron (ae3bffe1) was deleted at 19:56 CST — likely accidental deletion during session interaction.
action: PCEC evt_104 recreated the cron job with identical prompt and configuration (new ID: 5749f0f0).
notes: Project check has run successfully 3 consecutive times (12:00, 18:00 03-09, 12:00 03-10) with 11s completion. Converged.

signal: DeepRouter余额监控 timed out at 300s. Log shows 'Thinking level xhigh not supported for sonnet-4-6; downgrading to high' despite no thinking field in job config. FailoverError: LLM request timed out at 1244s.
action: Identified likely global agent default thinking level being applied to cron jobs without explicit thinking. Need to investigate agents.defaults config. Single occurrence so far (17:00 run OK, 17:30 failed).
notes: First occurrence. May be transient or related to global thinking default. DeepRouter succeeded at 17:00 (13s) and failed at 17:30 (300s timeout). The 'xhigh' downgrade to 'high' is suspicious — possibly from agents.defaults.
